rulings.Case Title: LEONARD SHIELDS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: First Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Shortening Time To Hear Defendants Motion For Protective Order Limiting Or Striking Plaintiffs Experts
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, February 5, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. DEFENDANTS VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC. and REYNOLD C. JOHNSON CO.'s motion for protective order limiting or striking plaintiff's experts is granted in part and denied in part. Defendants' request to strike plaintiffs' expert witness list on the grounds that the list was untimely served is denied. Other than health care professionals who provided treatment to plaintiffs who may testify as experts on behalf of plaintiffs, defendants are granted a protective order limiting the persons who plaintiffs may call as expert witness, to those persons who (1) were included in both the plaintiffs' "case-specific designation of expert witnesses" and "plaintiffs' expert witness declaration" (2) have been offered for deposition (3) are likely to testify at trial in the Shields case and (4) whose professional specialties are not the same as any other expert who meets the first three criteria.. Accordingly, defendants' request to strike experts Dr. Samuel Hammer and Dr. Barry Ben-Zion is granted as they were not included in the' expert witness declaration. Plaintiffs shall notify defendants no later than the end of the day February 8, 2010 of the one pulmonologist, one radiologist, one pathologist and one epidemiologist among those listed as experts in those areas in their expert declaration who are likely to testify at trial and will be offered for deposition. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR TEMPORARY PROTECTIVE ORDER, PERMANENT PROTECTIVE ORDER
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, February 5, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Utility Trailer Manufacturing's motion for protective order limiting or striking plaintiff's experts is granted in part and denied in part. Defendant's request to strike plaintiffs' expert witness list on the grounds that the list was untimely served is denied. Other than health care professionals who provided treatment to plaintiffs who may testify as experts on behalf of plaintiffs, defendant is granted a protective order limiting the persons who plaintiffs may call as expert witness, to those persons who (1) were included in both the plaintiffs' "case-specific designation of expert witnesses" and "plaintiffs' expert witness declaration" (2) have been offered for deposition (3) are likely to testify at trial in the Shields case and (4) whose professional specialties are not the same as any other expert who meets the first three criteria.. Accordingly, defendant's request to strike experts Dr. Samuel Hammer and Dr. Barry Ben-Zion is granted as they were not included in the' expert witness declaration. Plaintiffs shall notify defendant no later than the end of the day February 8, 2010 of the one pulmonologist, one radiologist, one pathologist and one epidemiologist among those listed as experts in those areas in their expert declaration who are likely to testify at trial and will be offered for deposition. =(220/HEK)
